title: Tracing genes to their ancestor
author: OpenDiscover
blurb: Take 20 copies of a gene and walk their family tree backwards in time until they all meet at one common ancestor. The Kingman coalescent says the waiting times explode as lineages run out: with 20 left they merge fast, but the final pair (T₂) alone takes on average half the total — the plotted waiting-time curve rockets up at the end. Expected time to the MRCA is 2(1−1/n) ≈ 1.9 coalescent units; total branch length 2·Σ1/k. Under θ=4Nμ=5 those branches carry ~θ·Σ1/k segregating sites, the raw material behind Watterson's estimator and every test for selection.
